items.name: Broadcom
items.note: jumped on $30B multi-year custom-silicon deal with Apple

items.name: Oracle
items.note: still well off its June AI-guidance highs; flagged data-center cost/delay risk on July 1

items.name: Micron Technology
items.note: hit by today's global chip selloff after SK Hynix slide in Korea

items.name: Tesla (Optimus)
items.note: UBS raised price target to $442 on physical AI/Optimus

items.name: Bitcoin
items.note: slipped below $63k in an Asian-session leverage flush

outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: Broadcom shares jumped roughly 4% after Apple confirmed a multi-year, $30B+ custom-silicon partnership expansion. It's a concrete sign that AI capex is diversifying beyond Nvidia into custom chip programs, and Wall Street's average price target on AVGO still sits well above current levels.

CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, Zscaler, and Okta are all up more than 20% since their June 25 low, and CRWD specifically has pushed further clear of its tracked stop. AI is expanding the attack surface as fast as it expands productivity, which keeps security spend relatively insulated from broader tech jitters.

weakest.reason: A slide in SK Hynix shares in Korea triggered a global chip selloff, with Sandisk and Micron both down sharply in premarket trade. Investors are questioning whether this year's AI-driven semiconductor rally has already priced in future growth, and rising oil prices tied to renewed Middle East tension are adding to the cautious mood.

Bitcoin slipped below $63k in an Asian-session leverage flush even as Ethereum diverged higher, up about 2%. That split — rather than a uniform risk-off move — is worth noting, along with U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs still logging positive weekly net inflows despite the dip.

outlook.nextStep: Broadcom's Apple deal is worth a closer look since it points to AI capex diversifying beyond Nvidia into custom silicon; the chip/memory selloff triggered by SK Hynix is better watched than acted on until it's clearer whether this is a healthy pause after a big rally or a deeper valuation reassessment. Zscaler's proximity to its tracked stop ($139.27 vs. $138.60) is also worth a check before the next session.

signals.title: Rate-hike odds climb as Iran conflict reignites, oil jumps 4%
signals.body: The Fed held its target rate at 3.50%-3.75% and is expected to hold again at the July 28-29 meeting, but renewed U.S.-Iran tension pushed WTI crude up over 4% to around $74 and lifted September rate-hike odds toward 70% in some futures pricing, as energy-driven inflation risk re-enters the conversation. Core PCE inflation hit 3.4% in May, the fastest pace in nearly three years.
signals.watch: The July 28-29 FOMC decision and whether oil holds above $74 into that meeting.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — this is the swing factor for growth-stock valuations across every other tier this month.

signals.title: Global chip selloff: SK Hynix slide drags Micron, Sandisk lower
signals.body: A sharp decline for SK Hynix in Korea triggered a broader slide in AI chip names, with Sandisk down roughly 6.8% and Micron down roughly 5.3% in premarket trade. The pullback follows a strong Samsung earnings report that failed to lift sentiment, as investors debate whether this year's AI-driven semiconductor rally already priced in the sector's future growth.
signals.watch: Whether the selloff is a short-lived valuation reset or the start of a broader reassessment of AI memory demand.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now on chasing memory names into the dip — worth letting the selloff stabilize before drawing conclusions.

signals.title: Broadcom lands $30B custom-silicon expansion with Apple
signals.body: Apple confirmed the financial details of an expanded multi-year partnership with Broadcom worth more than $30 billion, sending AVGO shares up roughly 4-5%. Apple shares also ticked higher on the news. The deal underscores custom AI silicon as a growing, diversified leg of the AI infrastructure buildout beyond merchant GPU suppliers.
signals.watch: Any read-through to other hyperscalers' custom-silicon roadmaps and Broadcom's next AI-revenue guidance update.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look as a sign AI capex is broadening out, not just concentrating in one supplier.

signals.title: Palantir gets a bullish call as Salesforce's Slack-AI push continues
signals.body: Palantir has drawn renewed analyst attention on its 'sovereign AI' positioning even as the stock remains down sharply year-to-date. Separately, Salesforce's recently shipped Slack-to-CRM integration tying together Data 360 and Tableau continues to be watched as a concrete product step in the agentic-AI-vs-SaaS debate.
signals.watch: Whether the Slack integration shows up in Salesforce's next seat-count and revenue commentary, and any follow-through on Palantir's recent analyst upgrades.
signals.focus: Worth watching for early proof points; the sector-wide re-rating pressure on AI software hasn't lifted yet.

signals.title: Mixed robotics tape: Tesla price-target hike vs. a rough six-month stretch
signals.body: UBS's Tesla price-target hike to $442 (from $364) on physical-AI/Optimus optimism stands out against a broader robotics sector that has averaged a -21.5% six-month return. Serve Robotics is pivoting from food delivery into healthcare automation via the Diligent Robotics acquisition, while Intuitive Surgical continues to benefit from FDA clearance of da Vinci 5 for additional procedures.
signals.watch: Any concrete Optimus production or external-sale numbers, and how Serve Robotics' healthcare pivot is received.
signals.focus: Speculative — the sector's dispersion between story stocks and delivered numbers is wide right now.

signals.title: Bitcoin slips below $63k while Ethereum diverges higher
signals.body: Bitcoin fell below $63,000 in an Asian-session leverage flush, though liquidations were only about a sixth of the worst levels seen in the past 30 days. Ethereum moved in the opposite direction, up about 2.15% to roughly $1,788. U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs still logged positive weekly net inflows, led by BlackRock, suggesting institutional demand remains intact despite the pullback.
signals.watch: Whether BTC and ETH continue to diverge, and any further escalation in Middle East tensions that could add to risk-off flows.
signals.focus: Worth checking in on — the BTC/ETH split and resilient ETF flows are more informative here than the daily price move alone.

signals.title: Zscaler drifts back toward its tracked stop as sector cools
signals.body: ZS fell roughly 5% to $139.27, edging close to its tracked stop at $138.60, even as the broader cybersecurity group (CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, Zscaler, Okta) remains up more than 20% since its June 25 low. CrowdStrike itself moved further above its own tracked stop, so the pullback looks more stock-specific than sector-wide so far. NetApp and Unity were comparatively quiet.
signals.watch: Whether $138.60 holds on ZS, and whether CRWD's relative strength continues to diverge from it.
signals.focus: Worth checking daily until ZS either reclaims ground above $144 or confirms the stop.
